mnemonics: My Very Eager Mother Just Served Us Nine Pizzas (but securely): A mnemonic for the planets adapted to remember that RSA relies on the difficulty of factoring large primes, while Diffie-Hellman relies on the difficulty of the Discrete Logarithm Problem.
keyPoints: RSA is an asymmetric algorithm used for secure data transmission, relying on the practical difficulty of factoring the product of two large prime numbers.
The Diffie-Hellman key exchange is a method for securely exchanging cryptographic keys over a public channel, based on the properties of modular exponentiation and the discrete logarithm problem.
A fundamental difference is that RSA can be used for both encryption and creating digital signatures, while Diffie-Hellman is solely a key-exchange protocol to establish a shared secret.
commonMistakes: Confusing the mathematical 'hard problems' each protocol is based on (Factoring for RSA vs. Discrete Log for Diffie-Hellman).
Believing Diffie-Hellman itself encrypts data; it only establishes a shared secret key which must then be used by another algorithm for encryption.
